heading: Precedence accorded commanding officers
label: 8166 Precedence accorded commanding officers
text: The commanding officer of a vessel or of a naval station takes precedence over all officers under his command.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5946.34 U.S.C. 246R.S. 1468.The word “placed” is omitted as surplusage. The words “of war” are omitted to avoid an erroneous implication that the section does not apply to the commanding officers of noncombatant ships of the Navy. As of the date of enactment of R.S. 1468, all vessels of the Navy were “vessels of war”; the elimination of the words, therefore, preserves the purpose of the statute.
